crime.value: Identity theft and imposture - a man assumes the identity of Martin Guerre through deception
crime.quote: confessed his imposture at great length

victim.value: Martin Guerre (the real one who died in battle) and Bertrande (his wife who was deceived)
victim.quote: He saw him fall, carried him away, and then...excited his delirium to the utmost to obtain possession of his secrets

closed_world.value: The village of Artigues and its surrounding community where everyone knew Martin Guerre
closed_world.quote: the populace, collecting from the whole neighbourhood, had assembled before the parish church of Artigues

culprit_and_motive.value: An unnamed impostor who assumed Martin's identity after watching him die in battle, motivated by desire for his life and wife
culprit_and_motive.quote: having returned from the camp in Picardy, he was addressed as Martin Guerre by several intimate friends

resolution.value: The impostor confesses, is executed, and justice is restored through legal proceedings and punishment
resolution.quote: the guilty man had just been executed in front of the house

investigator.value: Pierre Guerre (who made accusations) and the judge of Rieux who interrogated the impostor
investigator.quote: he was interrogated in prison by the judge of Rieux

suspect_architecture.value: Two-layer deception: the impostor Arnaud du Tilh as primary perpetrator, with village community (including Pierre Guerre as accuser) forming secondary suspects through their complicity or opposition
suspect_architecture.quote: Pierre had made, her own observations which had confirmed it, all her grief and trouble

red_herring.value: Supernatural explanation - the suggestion that the devil or magical forces created the resemblance to deceive Bertrande, deliberately planted to misdirect from human imposture
red_herring.quote: the Enemy himself could assume human form, and could borrow the semblance of a dead man

clue_fairness.value: Physical and behavioral inconsistencies in the impostor are presented to reader through Bertrande's observations before any formal investigation reveals the truth
clue_fairness.quote: her own observations which had confirmed it

social_world.value: Rural French village hierarchy with family patriarch Pierre Guerre holding authority to challenge identities, and community complicity in either accepting or rejecting the imposture
social_world.quote: She asked if Pierre Guerre had been to see her, and found he had not been near the house

alibi.value: The impostor's constructed identity as the returning Martin Guerre, supported by intimate knowledge extracted from the dying real Martin in battle
alibi.quote: excited his delirium to the utmost in order to obtain possession of his secrets

reveal_mechanic.value: Confession under interrogation combined with explanation of the deception method - battlefield impersonation and extraction of personal secrets from the dying victim
reveal_mechanic.quote: confessed his imposture at great length

media_and_audience.value: Public spectacle of penance and execution before the parish church with assembled populace, transforming private deception into community moral theater
media_and_audience.quote: the populace, collecting from the whole neighbourhood, had assembled before the parish church

investigator_wound.value: Bertrande's impossible position as both victim and complicit party - she cannot expose the truth without destroying herself and her child
investigator_wound.quote: she could not ruin him without ruining herself

_meta.title: ebook alexandre dumas pere martin guerre
